subtitle: A 10-km strip on the northern tip of Vypin Island where the Arabian Sea runs alongside the Kerala backwaters in one frame — and where, a few kilometres south, the same sea has been swallowing houses behind geotextile sacks and boulder walls.
spike_statement: Cherai is one of the few places you can see the Kerala backwaters and the open Arabian Sea in one view; the same low coast erodes fast just south.
